title: Dip Into National Cheese Fondue Day!
story: Bubbling pots of melted cheese take center stage every April 11 during National Cheese Fondue Day! People gather around fondue pots, dipping chunks of bread, vegetables, and fruit into gooey, delicious cheese mixtures. title: A Handshake Across Nations
story: Quills scratched on parchment as seven powerful countries made peace. In 1713, on April 11, the Treaty of Utrecht was signed. Great Britain, Spain, the Netherlands, Prussia, Savoy, Portugal, and France all agreed to end their fighting. As part of the deal, France gave up its Maritime provinces in North America to Britain, reshaping the map of the New World.
activity_question: Which country gave up its Maritime provinces in North America?
follow_up_question: Can you name three of the seven countries that signed the Treaty of Utrecht?

title: Fists Fly in First International Prizefight
story: Fists flew as Jack Slack battled Jean Petit in a historic boxing match. On April 11, 1750, England and France clashed in the ring for what's considered the first international prizefight. After seven intense rounds in Harlston, England, Slack emerged victorious. He kept his Champion of England title, proving his skills against fighters from beyond British shores.
activity_question: Who won the historic boxing match?
follow_up_question: Why was this boxing match considered special?

title: A College Grows from Church Basement
story: Two brave teachers started a school for Black women in a church basement. It was the beginning of Spelman College in Atlanta. The tiny school opened its doors on April 11, 1881. At first, it was called Atlanta Baptist Female Seminary. Today, Spelman is one of America's top colleges for women.
activity_question: What was Spelman College originally called?

title: Surprise Tennis Triumph at Athens Olympics
story: Rackets swung and balls flew as John Boland, an Irish tennis player, shocked everyone at the 1896 Athens Olympics. Representing Great Britain, he smashed his way to victory in both the men's singles and doubles finals on April 11. His opponent in both matches, Dionysios Kasdaglis from Greece, fought hard but couldn't overcome Boland's skill on the court.
activity_question: Who did John Boland represent in the 1896 Athens Olympics?
bonus_fact: The 1896 Athens Olympics were the first modern Olympic Games.

title: Rifkabylen Triumph in Morocco
story: Fierce warriors clashed in the rugged mountains of Morocco. On April 11, 1925, the Rifkabylen fighters, led by Abd el-Krim, scored a surprising victory against the French army. The Rifkabylen used their knowledge of the terrain and guerrilla tactics to outmaneuver the larger French force. This battle became a symbol of resistance for the local people.

title: Helicopter Pioneer Takes Flight
story: Whirling blades sliced the air as Frank Piasecki made history. On April 11, 1943, this American helicopter designer took his first single-rotor craft skyward. Piasecki's successful flight marked a huge step for aviation. He would go on to found Vertol, a company that revolutionized helicopter design. His innovations helped pave the way for the choppers we see today.

title: Ireland Casts a Wider Net
story: Fishermen cheered as Ireland expanded its fishing zone in 1977. On April 11, the country set a new boundary stretching 50 miles from its shores. This move gave Irish boats more room to catch fish without competition from other nations. It was a big change that helped protect Ireland's sea life and support local fishing communities.
activity_question: What did Ireland set at 50 miles?
bonus_fact: Fish don't recognize borders, but they do follow ocean currents and temperatures!

title: Red Wings Soar to Hockey History
story: The Detroit Red Wings skated into the record books in 1996. On April 11, they became only the second team in National Hockey League history to win 60 games in a single season. This incredible achievement showcased the Red Wings' skill, teamwork, and determination. Hockey fans across Detroit celebrated their team's amazing performance.
activity_question: What did the Detroit Red Wings achieve in 1996?
